task: Methodology navigation rail continuous motion fix

root_cause.summary: The prior rail implementation could remain paused after interaction because hover, focus, manual scroll, and a delayed resume timeout all fed the same paused state. The scroll handler could re-pause the rail after hover ended, making the movement resume briefly and then stop.
root_cause.affected_behavior: Methodology rail auto-scroll paused on hover but did not keep moving continuously after unhover.

implementation.auto_scroll_loop: Uses one mounted animation loop that keeps scheduling frames while the component is mounted. The loop does not exit when paused.
implementation.fallback_loop: Uses a recursive window.setTimeout loop only when requestAnimationFrame is unavailable.
implementation.pause_resume_behavior: Pauses only while the pointer is over the methodology rail. Pointer leave clears the hover pause immediately and resets frame timing so movement resumes without a jump.
removed_pause_sources: shared pausedRef state that could remain true after hover
implementation.boundary_behavior: At the right edge the rail reverses left; at the left edge it reverses right. It does not reset or stop at the edges.
implementation.reduced_motion: prefers-reduced-motion is observed. When enabled, the loop stays mounted but does not auto-scroll; manual scrolling remains available.
implementation.scrollbar_behavior: Native scrollbar remains hidden with scrollbar-width: none and hidden WebKit scrollbar. Rail scroll-behavior is auto so per-frame scrollLeft updates are not interrupted by smooth-scroll interpolation.

dev_cfs_notes: Frontend health returned HTTP 200 at http://localhost:3000.
FastAPI health returned HTTP 200 at http://127.0.0.1:8000.
Built-in dev:cfs endpoint checks passed for parcels, development, flood, and school endpoints.

hover_pause_check.note: The in-app browser coordinate move/click did not produce a browser :hover chain for the rail, so pointer hover could not be faithfully simulated. Code now pauses exclusively from onPointerEnter and resumes from onPointerLeave.

remaining_known_issues: Browser automation could not simulate actual pointer hover on the rail, but live motion, hidden scrollbar, mode switching, and prediction-safety checks passed.
No unrelated dashboard areas were modified.
